settings.title: How Does VAT Affect the Body?
settings.text: The mechanism of action of VAT is the oscillation of the human nervous system with frequencies and resonance characteristics of various muscle groups. VAT combines these resonance frequencies programs based on experimentally based effects, evaluated by patient reactions. Thousands of research hours have taught us how to use these programs to relax, stimulate and relieve pain.

settings.title: Who Can Benefit From VAT?
settings.text:
VAT may be an independent therapy or incorporated with medical
treatment and psychotherapy. As a natural energy booster and pain killer that is composed of varieties of frequencies therapy solutions that can be used as a self-management tool, it is beneficial to people with chronic or special needs, and any person with stress that wants to apply preventive and integrative
wellness therapy. 

VAT has been studied by researchers worldwide including the National Institutes of Health (NIH). NIH reports the benefits of using VAT for Parkinson’s, PTSD, anxiety, physical injuries, brain function, COPD, Asthma and much more.

settings.title: Some benefits of VAT include:
settings.text:
•  Increase sleep quality

  •  Reduce or eliminate effects of stress

  •  Address PTSD / somatic ( physical) symptoms

  •  Regulate the limbic system (emotional memories)

  •  Activate the Relaxation Response

  •  Reduce anxiety and muscle pain or spasms

  •  Shorten healing periods/increase circulation

settings.title: Further reading
settings.text:
1.Campbell EA, Hynynen J, Burger B, Ala-Ruona E.

Exploring the use of Vibroacoustic treatment for managing chronic pain and comorbid mood disorders: A mixed methods study.

Nordic Journal of Music Therapy. 2019 Aug 8;28(4):291-314.

2.Weber A, Busbridge S, Governo R.
Evaluation of the Efficacy of Musical Vibroacupuncture in Pain Relief: A Randomized Controlled Pilot Study.

Neuromodulation: Technology at the Neural Interface. 2021 Dec 1;24(8):1475-82.

3.Bartel L, Mosabbir A.

Possible mechanisms for the effects of sound vibration on human health.

InHealthcare 2021 May 1 (Vol. 9, No. 5, p. 597). MDPI.

4.Clements-Cortes A, Bartel L.

Are we doing more than we know? possible mechanisms of response to music therapy.

Frontiers in medicine. 2018:255.

5.Mosabbir AA, Janzen TB, Al Shirawi M, Rotzinger S, Kennedy SH, Farzan F, Meltzer J, Bartel L.

Investigating the Effects of Auditory and Vibrotactile Rhythmic Sensory Stimulation on
Depression

An EEG Pilot Study. Cureus. 2022 Feb 24;14(2).

6.Kantor J, Campbell EA, Kantorová L, Marečková J, Regec V, Karasová K, Sedláčková D, Klugar M.

Exploring vibroacoustic therapy in adults experiencing pain: a scoping review.

BMJ open. 2022 Apr 1;12(4):e046591.
